post.headline: Add your encryption key before you share your tip line
post.subtext: The guided setup walks you through adding a public PGP key so sources can send messages that only you can read.
post.image_alt_text: Social graphic built around a mobile Hush Line onboarding screen. The screen shows step 2 of 4, titled "Now, let's set up encryption," with options to search for a Proton Mail key or add a public PGP key manually.

social.bluesky: Setting up Hush Line? Add your public PGP key during onboarding before you share your tip line. The updated setup flow keeps encryption in the main path, so sources can send messages that only you can read.
social.linkedin: Before you share a Hush Line profile, add your public PGP key. The guided setup now puts encryption in the main path, with Proton lookup or manual key entry in the same flow. That helps journalists, lawyers, educators, organizers, and other trusted recipients reach a usable tip line without skipping the step that lets sources send messages only they can read.
social.mastodon: If you're setting up Hush Line for reporting, legal work, education, or community use, don't skip encryption. The onboarding flow now walks you through adding your public PGP key, so sources can send messages that only you can read.

post.rationale: This screen reflects the recent auth and onboarding form modernization in PR #1743 and shows a core recipient task clearly above the fold. The docs make encryption a prerequisite for a usable tip line: recipients need a public PGP key before sources can submit messages that only the recipient can read.
post.copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.

matched_pull_requests.title: #1733 Phase 6: modernize auth and onboarding core form flows

post.title: Onboarding - Step 2 Encryption (newman)

summary: Feature the guided encryption step in onboarding, because it reflects the recent onboarding form rollout and a core recipient need before sharing a tip line.
